illinois.h2: What Illinois residents should know about the Paragard lawsuits
illinois.body:
Cases filed by Illinois residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-2974 before Judge Leigh Martin May in the Northern District of Georgia, where about 4,071 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026. The first bellwether trial ended in a defense verdict for Teva in February 2026, and additional bellwether trials are scheduled in 2026; no global settlement exists.

Illinois’s personal injury deadline is 2 years (735 ILCS 5/13-202). Because a broken Paragard is often discovered only when the device fractures during removal, accrual and discovery-rule questions can be important. Illinois courts apply a discovery rule in many injury cases, so the clock may run from when the injury and its likely cause were or should have been discovered. Product liability actions are also subject to statutory repose periods (735 ILCS 5/13-213). A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific Illinois claim.

faqs.answer: Federal Paragard cases filed by Illinois residents are transferred into MDL-2974 in the Northern District of Georgia before Judge Leigh Martin May. Illinois's personal injury deadline is 2 years (735 ILCS 5/13-202); because breakage is often discovered at removal, a lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies.

missouri.h2: What Missouri residents should know about the Paragard lawsuits
missouri.body:
Cases filed by Missouri residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-2974 before Judge Leigh Martin May in the Northern District of Georgia, where about 4,071 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026. The first bellwether trial ended in a defense verdict for Teva in February 2026, and additional bellwether trials are scheduled in 2026; no global settlement exists.

Missouri’s personal injury deadline is 5 years (Mo. Rev. Stat. § 516.120(4)). Because a broken Paragard is often discovered only when the device fractures during removal, accrual and discovery-rule questions can be important. Missouri's period generally runs from when damage is sustained and capable of ascertainment, which can function like a discovery rule in latent-injury cases. A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific Missouri claim.

texas.h2: What Texas residents should know about the Paragard lawsuits
texas.body:
Cases filed by Texas residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-2974 before Judge Leigh Martin May in the Northern District of Georgia, where about 4,071 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026. The first bellwether trial ended in a defense verdict for Teva in February 2026, and additional bellwether trials are scheduled in 2026; no global settlement exists.

Texas’s personal injury deadline is 2 years (Tex. Civ. Prac. & Rem. Code § 16.003). Because a broken Paragard is often discovered only when the device fractures during removal, accrual and discovery-rule questions can be important. Texas recognizes a discovery rule in limited circumstances involving inherently undiscoverable injuries. Texas applies a 15-year statute of repose to many product liability claims (§ 16.012). A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific Texas claim.

florida.h2: What Florida residents should know about the Paragard lawsuits
florida.body:
Cases filed by Florida residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-2974 before Judge Leigh Martin May in the Northern District of Georgia, where about 4,071 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026. The first bellwether trial ended in a defense verdict for Teva in February 2026, and additional bellwether trials are scheduled in 2026; no global settlement exists.

Florida’s personal injury deadline is 4 years (Fla. Stat. § 95.11(3)(d)). Because a broken Paragard is often discovered only when the device fractures during removal, accrual and discovery-rule questions can be important. Florida applies a four-year period to injury actions founded on the design, manufacture, distribution, or sale of personal property. Florida's 2023 tort reform (HB 837) cut the general negligence period to two years (§ 95.11(4)(a)) but did not change the product-liability period at § 95.11(3)(d). Latent-injury accrual rules can apply. Florida product liability claims can be subject to a 12-year repose period for certain products (§ 95.031). A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific Florida claim.

california.h2: What California residents should know about the Paragard lawsuits
california.body:
Cases filed by California residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-2974 before Judge Leigh Martin May in the Northern District of Georgia, where about 4,071 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026. The first bellwether trial ended in a defense verdict for Teva in February 2026, and additional bellwether trials are scheduled in 2026; no global settlement exists.

California’s personal injury deadline is 2 years (Cal. Code Civ. Proc. § 335.1). Because a broken Paragard is often discovered only when the device fractures during removal, accrual and discovery-rule questions can be important. California applies a discovery rule: the period generally runs from when a plaintiff discovers, or reasonably should have discovered, the injury and its wrongful cause. A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific California claim.

north-carolina.h2: What North Carolina residents should know about the Paragard lawsuits
north-carolina.body:
Cases filed by North Carolina residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-2974 before Judge Leigh Martin May in the Northern District of Georgia, where about 4,071 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026. The first bellwether trial ended in a defense verdict for Teva in February 2026, and additional bellwether trials are scheduled in 2026; no global settlement exists.

North Carolina’s personal injury deadline is 3 years (N.C. Gen. Stat. § 1-52). Because a broken Paragard is often discovered only when the device fractures during removal, accrual and discovery-rule questions can be important. North Carolina applies discovery-based accrual to many latent injury claims (§ 1-52(16)). North Carolina applies a 12-year statute of repose to product liability actions (§ 1-46.1). A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific North Carolina claim.

georgia.h2: Why Paragard cases are centered in Georgia
georgia.body:
Georgia is the home of this litigation: the federal MDL-2974 is centralized in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Georgia (Atlanta) before Judge Leigh Martin May, where about 4,071 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026. The first bellwether trial there ended in a defense verdict for Teva in February 2026, after which the court weighed revising the bellwether plan; additional bellwether trials are scheduled in 2026.

Georgia’s personal injury deadline is two years (O.C.G.A. § 9-3-33), and Georgia also applies a 10-year statute of repose to many product-liability claims (O.C.G.A. § 51-1-11(b)(2)) — a potentially decisive limit for older device claims. A lawyer can confirm how these deadlines apply to a specific Georgia claim.

faqs.question: Why are Paragard cases centered in Georgia?
faqs.answer: The federal Paragard MDL (MDL-2974) is centralized in the Northern District of Georgia before Judge Leigh Martin May, making Georgia the home court for the litigation. Georgia's personal injury deadline is two years (O.C.G.A. Section 9-3-33), subject to a 10-year product-liability statute of repose.

ohio.h2: What Ohio residents should know about the Paragard lawsuits
ohio.body:
Cases filed by Ohio residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-2974 before Judge Leigh Martin May in the Northern District of Georgia, where about 4,071 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026. The first bellwether trial ended in a defense verdict for Teva in February 2026, and additional bellwether trials are scheduled in 2026; no global settlement exists.

Ohio’s personal injury deadline is 2 years (Ohio Rev. Code § 2305.10). Because a broken Paragard is often discovered only when the device fractures during removal, accrual and discovery-rule questions can be important. Ohio's statute includes discovery-based accrual provisions for certain exposure-related claims. Ohio applies a 10-year statute of repose to many product liability claims (§ 2305.10(C)). A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific Ohio claim.

pennsylvania.h2: What Pennsylvania residents should know about the Paragard lawsuits
pennsylvania.body:
Cases filed by Pennsylvania residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-2974 before Judge Leigh Martin May in the Northern District of Georgia, where about 4,071 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026. The first bellwether trial ended in a defense verdict for Teva in February 2026, and additional bellwether trials are scheduled in 2026; no global settlement exists.

Pennsylvania’s personal injury deadline is 2 years (42 Pa. Cons. Stat. § 5524). Because a broken Paragard is often discovered only when the device fractures during removal, accrual and discovery-rule questions can be important. Pennsylvania applies a discovery rule that can delay accrual until the injury and its cause reasonably could have been discovered. A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific Pennsylvania claim.

michigan.h2: What Michigan residents should know about the Paragard lawsuits
michigan.body:
Cases filed by Michigan residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-2974 before Judge Leigh Martin May in the Northern District of Georgia, where about 4,071 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026. The first bellwether trial ended in a defense verdict for Teva in February 2026, and additional bellwether trials are scheduled in 2026; no global settlement exists.

Michigan’s personal injury deadline is 3 years (Mich. Comp. Laws § 600.5805(2)). Because a broken Paragard is often discovered only when the device fractures during removal, accrual and discovery-rule questions can be important. Michigan applies limited discovery-based accrual rules; latent-injury treatment varies by claim type. A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific Michigan claim.
